type_error_policy: Pycairo runtime TypeError and AttributeError assertions map to MoonBit compile-time rejection. Rectangle is a closed typed value with four required Double constructor arguments and four public fields; tuple subclassing maps to fields, components, checked indexing, value equality, and hashing. Python repr/eval behavior maps to the derived structural Debug representation and typed construction rather than runtime code evaluation.

static_api: pub fn Rectangle::new(Double, Double, Double, Double) -> Self
pub fn Rectangle::components(Self) -> (Double, Double, Double, Double)
pub fn Rectangle::at(Self, Int) -> Double raise CairoError
test_type.adaptation: Rectangle is a closed MoonBit struct rather than a Python tuple subclass. Fields, components, equality, hashing, checked index syntax, and derived Debug preserve the useful value protocol; missing constructor arguments, unknown fields, and heterogeneous tuple equality are rejected statically.

runtime: src/tests/value/pycairo/value_pycairo_parity_test.mbt::pycairo rectangle producing APIs return rectangle values
static_api: pub fn Context::copy_clip_rectangle_list(Self) -> Array[Rectangle] raise CairoError

runtime: src/tests/value/pycairo/value_pycairo_parity_test.mbt::pycairo rectangle producing APIs return rectangle values
static_api: pub fn Surface::recording(Content, extents? : Rectangle?) -> Self raise CairoError
pub fn Surface::recording_get_extents(Self) -> Rectangle? raise CairoError
test_recording_surface.adaptation: Pycairo uses a nullable recording extent return; MoonBit represents it explicitly as Rectangle?.
